name: Tex-Mex Beans with Cornmeal Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 15 oz Can Red Kidney Beans, drained
4 oz Can diced green chili pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix together the flour, cornmeal, baking powder, and 1/4 teaspoon salt until combined.
2. In a small bowl, beat together the egg white, milk, and oil until smooth.
3. Heat the cooking oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and minced garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are tender, about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in the garbanzo beans, red kidney beans, tomato sauce, drained green chili peppers, chili powder, and salt to taste. Cook for a few minutes until the vegetables are well coated with the spices.
5. In another small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and 1 tablespoon water. Add this mixture to the bean mixture in the skillet. Stir continuously over medium heat until the sauce thickens slightly and begins to bubble.
6. For the dumplings, mix the beaten egg white, milk, cooking oil, salt, and remaining 1/4 cup cornmeal in a separate bowl until just combined.
7. Spoon spoonfuls of the dumpling mixture onto the top of the bean mixture, creating about five mounds.
8. Cover the skillet and continue to cook for 10-12 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a dumpling comes out clean.
Per serving: 313 calories, 13 g protein, 50 g carbohydrates, 8 g fat, 0 mg cholesterol, 1023 mg sodium, 839 mg potassium.
